// featured work

Projects

10 real-world dashboards and analyses spanning music, retail, healthcare, food, and business intelligence.

🎵
Spotify Dashboard
Music Analytics · Power BI

Analysed Spotify streaming history 2013–2024 — top artists, track skipping patterns, listening time-of-day behaviour and new music discovery ratios.

Night listening dominates at 47.58% of total streams, with The Beatles leading artist play time by a wide margin.
September has the highest ratio of new music discovery (3.75), suggesting seasonal openness to new artists.
"Ode to the Mets" is the most-played track with the fewest skips, indicating strong listener loyalty to the song.
Power BIDAXData Modelling
🐙 View in GitHub 📊 View in Power BI
🍕
Pizza Place Sales
Food & Beverage · Power BI

Sales performance analysis for a pizza restaurant — 817K+ total sales, order volume by hour, top-selling pizzas and monthly revenue trends across a full calendar year.

Peak ordering hours fall between 12 PM–2 PM and 6 PM–8 PM, with midday driving the highest single-hour order count.
The Barbecue Chicken pizza consistently ranks as the top revenue-generating item across all months.
Monthly sales remain relatively stable (~65K–70K), with slight upticks in July and November suggesting seasonal demand.
Power BISQLExcel
🐙 View in GitHub 📊 View in Power BI
🥤
Coca-Cola Retailer Sales Dashboard
Retail Analytics · Excel

Comprehensive dashboard tracking $4.38M in Coca-Cola retailer sales across 5 US regions — profit margins, brand share, retailer rankings and state-level growth opportunities.

The West region dominates total sales at over $1.2M, significantly outperforming all other regions including Northeast and Midwest.
Coca-Cola leads beverage brand sales across all regions, with the Southeast recording the highest brand units at 2,300.3 — nearly doubling some competitors.
Amazon, BevCo and FizzyCo are the top 3 retailers tracked, with state-level filters (Texas, Utah, Vermont) enabling targeted regional performance deep-dives.
ExcelPower QueryPivotTables
🐙 View in GitHub 📗 View in Excel
🏪
Northwind Traders
Business Intelligence · Power BI

End-to-end BI analysis of the Northwind relational database — $570K in total sales, monthly revenue trends, top customer companies, best-selling products and freight performance by company.

QUICK-Stop is the top customer at $110K, with March generating the highest monthly sales of $177K.
Camembert Pierrot and Raclette Courdavault are the two highest-quantity products ordered across all time periods.
Sales dip sharply in May–June ($36K–$72K), suggesting a seasonal trough that could inform promotional strategies.
Power BISQLDAX
🐙 View in GitHub 📊 View in Power BI
💻
HP Sales Dashboard
Tech Sales Analytics · Excel

Multi-chart Excel dashboard analysing HP product sales of $96.27M profit across regions, product lines and salesperson performance — with 5 analytical question-driven visuals.

The East region is the highest-performing sales and profit region, consistently outperforming North, South and West.
The top 3 salespersons by profit are Grace, Efc and Musa — with laptops and monitors driving the bulk of revenue.
Total units sold reached $100.2M, with profit peaking mid-year before declining — pointing to seasonal sales cycles.
ExcelPower QuerySales Analysis
🐙 View in GitHub 📗 View in Excel
🏬
Superstore Retail Dashboard
Retail Analytics · Tableau

Tableau dashboard analysing Superstore sales across Furniture, Office Supplies and Technology — including segment performance, regional distribution and monthly sales trend lines.

Technology leads category sales with $836K total; the Consumer segment accounts for the largest share at $1.16M.
Sales show a strong upward trend from M07 onwards, peaking at $352K in November before a slight December dip.
Furniture sub-categories include both high-performers and loss-makers — requiring targeted margin improvement strategies.
TableauExcelRetail Analysis
🐙 View in GitHub 📈 View in Tableau
🏥
Healthcare Appointment Dashboard
Healthcare Analytics · Tableau

Tableau dashboard tracking patient appointments, department revenue, doctor workloads, visit type distribution, patient age groups and wait time scatter analysis.

Pediatrics generates the highest department revenue, while Dr. Ahmed and Dr. Chika carry the heaviest appointment workloads.
58% of visits are follow-up appointments — highlighting the need for efficient repeat-patient scheduling and resource allocation.
Appointment volume drops sharply after Day 25 of the month, indicating end-of-month scheduling gaps that could be optimised.
TableauSQLHealthcare
🐙 View in GitHub 📈 View in Tableau
🍽️
Restaurant Analysis
Food & Beverage · Python / Pandas

Python and Pandas analysis of 12,230 restaurant order records across Q1 2023 — identifying peak times, highest-spending orders, most/least ordered items and category performance.

Peak order times cluster around late morning (11:38 AM) with consistent multi-item orders, suggesting a strong brunch-hour rush.
A single order (ID #2) contained 4 items ordered simultaneously — indicating group dining as a significant revenue driver.
Analysis covered 5,369 unique orders over 90 days, providing a statistically reliable base for menu and staffing decisions.
PythonPandasMatplotlib
🐙 View in GitHub
📦
Inventory Management Analysis
Operations Analytics · SQL

MySQL database built from scratch — Products and Sales tables with foreign key relationships, sample product data across Electronics, Apparel, Furniture and Footwear categories.

Designed a normalised schema with Products and Sales tables linked via foreign key constraints, ensuring data integrity.
Product categories span Electronics ($500), Apparel ($40), Furniture ($200) and Footwear ($90) — enabling price-tier analysis.
SQL INSERT statements structured to simulate real inventory flows, providing a reusable template for stock management queries.
SQLMySQLOperations
🐙 View in GitHub
🗃️
TFQ Database Analysis
Database Analytics · SQL

Advanced MySQL querying on a sales database — multi-level subqueries, HAVING clauses, aggregate functions and CTEs to identify stores performing above the company average in total sales.

Used nested subqueries with AVG() and SUM() to dynamically filter stores whose total sales exceed the cross-store average.
Implemented two equivalent query approaches (HAVING vs JOIN) to validate results and demonstrate SQL problem-solving depth.
Analysis of store_id and store_name groupings reveals which locations drive disproportionate revenue — key for resource planning.
SQLMySQLData Analysis
🐙 View in GitHub
